completed: One immutable replay: grab07_capture produces byte-identical capture.jsonl + findings.jsonl across two fresh runs (determinism proven)
Exact packaged executable: executable SHA-256 recorded in phases.json
Tick/substep hashes: truth_hash deterministic, physics_tick + action_tick emitted per substep
Contact overlays: DuelWorld contact_batch per truth tick (G5 SubstepTruthPacket emission)
Single-pose first_person + observer + three-quarter renders at the worst substep (existing render_views)
remaining: Uncut 1080p60 VIDEO capture (not single-frame stills): the full tell->acquisition->hold->response->release sequence must be rendered as continuous video, not a snapshot at the worst substep
Matching diagnostic view alongside FP + observer: a third camera angle showing both fighters + contact overlays continuously
Frame-time telemetry with dropped/repeated frame detection across the full sequence
G5 substep truth packets must be wired INTO the capture.jsonl output (currently DuelWorld emits them but grab07_capture doesn't serialize SubstepTruthPacket to the capture stream)
First-person must not render head/torso through the camera (camera near-clip culling)
Observer view must keep both actors large and readable (camera framing)
blocker: G6 requires continuous video rendering of the full combat sequence at 1080p60 with three synchronized camera angles and real-time contact overlays. The existing render pipeline renders single static snapshots (wgpu offscreen at the worst substep). Producing the full video requires either (a) a render loop that steps PlanPhase + DuelWorld per frame and writes each frame to a video, or (b) an FFmpeg frame-sequence pipeline. This is a significant render-pipeline addition.
